_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-29 Professional Standards Review Organization and Committees (Initial 07/02/2025, MDHHS Approved 07/25/2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-29: Professional Standards Review Organization and Committees — MDHHS Approved 7/25/2025
purpose: Establish a policy for appointing a Professional Standards Review Organization (PSRO) and committees by the Medical Control Authority and define PSRO authorities, composition, roles, and responsibilities.

sections.title: PSRO Authority and Structure
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-29: PSRO — Authority and Structure
points: MCAs must appoint a Professional Standards Review Organization under MCL 333.20919(1)(g) and administrative rules R 325.22202 and R 325.22207
All meetings of the PSRO are closed meetings
For this protocol, PSRO refers to the PSRO of the MCA
The PSRO has authority to request and compel pertinent records, information, data, recordings, and other materials related to EMS practice within the MCA jurisdiction
Requests for materials must be honored in accordance with MCA statutory obligations and turned over in a timely manner unless prohibited by law

PSRO committees must include at least one licensed prehospital EMS clinician, one participating Life Support Agency representative, MCA Board and/or MCA staff representative(s), and a representative from a participating MCA hospital

sections.title: PSRO Functions
points: The MCA Board is responsible for ensuring PSRO duties related to complaints, investigations, and QI activities are performed
PSRO may request and review EMS PCRs, hospital charts, data, audio, video, professional credentials, and other materials relevant to professional practice
PSRO assesses adherence to statutes, administrative rules, and protocols governing EMS practice
PSRO reviews and makes recommendations on professional standards and practices outlined in protocols
Incident reviews include unusual occurrences, protocol-required reviews, and complaints or allegations; complaints follow the PSRO Investigation and Resolution Protocol
QI activities include identifying needs and recommending training or development to enhance professional competencies
Mi-EMSIS data should be used as a tool to evaluate performance and function for quality improvement
PSRO responsibilities may include research projects, special studies, and audits

sections.title: Confidentiality
points: All records, data, and knowledge obtained for or by individuals assigned a professional review function of the PSRO are confidential
PSRO materials must not be made public or available for subpoena and are protected from disclosure under Michigan law, including 1967 PA 270 and MCL 333.20919(1)(g)
All PSRO members and subjects of a PSRO inquiry are subject to confidentiality obligations for materials and discussions reviewed
Participants may not remove materials from PSRO meetings or disclose materials or discussions outside the PSRO without appropriate authorization
The PSRO may disclose deidentified aggregate data for review by MCA committees, stakeholders, and the public

sections.title: MCA Committees and LSA Responsibilities
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-29: PSRO — Committees and LSA Responsibilities
points: MCAs may choose PSRO committees with specific responsibilities based on content expertise
Potential committees include Quality Review Committee, Systems of Care, Emergency Medical Dispatching, or others determined by the MCA Board
PSRO committee members are responsible for attending professional training activities as determined by the MCA Board or Department
Each Life Support Agency within the MCA must develop and maintain a PSRO subgroup for peer review functions and audits requested by the MCA
Each Life Support Agency must ensure staff are trained and provided current MCA protocols

scope_notes: PSRO means Professional Standards Review Organization
All PSRO meetings are closed meetings
PSRO may compel EMS-related records, data, recordings, and materials from MCA participants unless prohibited by law
Minimum committee composition includes licensed prehospital EMS clinician, Life Support Agency representative, MCA Board/staff representative, and MCA hospital representative
PSRO materials are confidential and protected from subpoena/disclosure under Michigan law
Deidentified aggregate data may be disclosed for committee, stakeholder, and public review
LSAs must maintain PSRO subgroup review functions and train staff on current MCA protocols
